content_brief.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Get It Back Fast
content_brief.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, state deadlines, and how to send a formal demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99.
content_outline: Introduction: The Frustration of a Withheld Security Deposit
Know Your Rights: State Deadlines and Return Requirements
What Landlords Can and Cannot Deduct (Normal Wear vs. Damage)
Why a Demand Letter Is Your Best First Move
How to Escalate to Small Claims Court If They Still Won’t Pay
Frequently Asked Questions About Security Deposit Returns
key_points: Most US states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days, or they forfeit the right to make deductions.
Normal wear and tear—such as faded paint, carpet wear, and small nail holes—cannot be legally deducted from a tenant's deposit.
A formal, state-specific demand letter is often legally required before a tenant can file a claim in small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com generates a professional security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 fee with no signup or subscription required.
content_brief.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription, no stress.
content_brief.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit after move-out.
